children.description: Extract title and body from raw markdown input

behavior.prose: Scan lines of raw for the first line that starts with '# '; strip the leading '# ' to produce title.
All lines other than the title line are joined (preserving order) to produce body.

children.description: Produce a content-analysis dict from the article body via four independent sub-analyses

behavior.prose: Count whitespace-separated words in body; store as analysis['word_count'] (int).
Split body on '.' to get sentences; compute average words-per-sentence; store analysis['reading_level'] = 'easy' if average < 12 else 'hard'.
Lowercase every word in body, count frequencies, pick the most frequent; break ties alphabetically; store as analysis['top_keyword'] (string).
Store analysis['has_links'] = true if the substring 'http' appears anywhere in body, else false.

The four sub-analyses (word_count, reading_level, top_keyword, has_links) each concern a separate property of the body and do not depend on each other; they are combined into a single analysis dict as their only point of coupling.

children.description: Assemble the final published record from title, body, and analysis

behavior.prose: Build published dict with keys: title (from input), body (from input), analysis (from input), and summary = '<title> (<word_count> words, <reading_level>)' where word_count and reading_level are drawn from analysis.
